creator: pkubaj@FreeBSD.org
text: Adding to what I wrote, you would need to invent another way of managing linux compatibility layer, like some script to install it, and another one to remove it. Of course, you're free to do it, just install CentOS in a chroot to /compat/linux and there install yum. You don't have to do it with the ports way, but don't complain when something doesn't work.
